channel.title: Posts on Chasing Dings!
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Chasing Dings!

item.title: Dominus Automa: An Idle MMO for Busy Dads
item.description: Someday it'll be an offline MMO for those with busy lives who want to text sweet nothings to their player character. Someday.

item.title: Erenshor v0.3: "Rising Shadows"
item.description: I hit max level in Erenshor, and BurgeeMedia immediately drops a whole new update full of cool stuff. Coincidence? Maybe!

item.title: Starfleet Academy: Your Billionaire Future
item.description: There's an interesting story in Starfleet Academy, but it's not a story billionaires would want to tell.

item.title: Quest 64: I played the worst RPG on the Nintendo 64 -- and I liked it
item.description: My quest to play every RPG on the Nintendo 64 continues with Quest 64, often called the worst RPG on the system. But is it?

item.title: Rubik and the Microplastic Mind
item.description: I just went to my first "cubing" competition -- the "Wide Moves and Waterslides CT" tournament held this weekend by Cube New England. It won't be the last,

item.title: Holiday Grab Bag: Dune, Palworld, Quest 64
item.description: We moved servers in Dune, killed a God pal in Palworld, and escaped the Blue Cave in Quest 64. Read on...

item.title: 2025, a Year in Pictures
item.description: Remembering 2025 through the pictures and screenshots I took throughout the year. There's a lot.

item.title: Steam Winter Sale: Seafarer, the Ship Sim
item.description: Dive into the world of commercial shipping as you patrol the seas and learn to love ISO 668 cargo containers.

item.title: Steam Winter Sale: Dispatch
item.description: Part superhero cartoon, part workplace comedy, part 911 Operator, this collaboration between ex-Telltale devs and Critical Role hits more than it misses.

item.title: It's a Vectrex Christmas Miracle!
item.description: Also Nintendo 64 and... EverQuest II? Yes, EQ2.

item.title: Widget's Workshop: Build Robots Better.
item.description: If you've been fomenting a drop-style card crafting revolution, this might be the game for you.

item.title: The Perfect Christmas Gift
item.description: Hint: It's not 3D printed puzzle boxes. But that's what they're getting, anyway.

item.title: The 2025 Daily Blogroll Awards
item.description: The (short) history of the Daily Blogroll, and a recognition of the best bloggers from the past three months, as judged by the LLM summarizer itself.

item.title: Prompting Malifaux
item.description: Why yes, I do enjoy making my Malifaux masters into chibis :-)

item.title: I Know What I Did Last Weekend
item.description: Time for another round-up of my recent gaming adventures.

item.title: DragonStrike in my kitchen?
item.description: We finally got the dragon aerial combat game to the table, and it was an unqualified hit.

item.title: HeroQuest First Light: The Ring of Return
item.description: Dragons to the left of us, Dread Warriors to the right, here I am, stuck in the middle with a dwarf, an elf, a rogue and a barbarian.

item.title: Return to Erenshor
item.description: It's been a few months since I last played, and now there's been a change that makes the wait worthwhile.

item.title: Analogue 3D: Play your Nintendo 64 games again
item.description: Analogue's latest retro console hardware emulator is a godsend for N64 afficionados, and almost nothing for anyone else.

item.title: Pokémon Legends: Z-A rolls credits
item.description: Is the latest entry in the Pokemon series worth buying a Switch 2 to play? Is it important to have played Pokemon X/Y first? These are questions!

item.title: Lost Worlds: not so lost anymore?
item.description: The "Lost Worlds" series of fantasy combat book games is being reprinted? Here in Connecticut? And I'm just finding out about this?

item.title: Octopath Traveler 0: You're the Octopath
item.description: In the third console release of the Octopath Traveler series, you become the hero who can rebuild your town.

item.title: You had ONE JOB, Dark Quest 4!
item.description: You had one job -- bring the HeroQuest board game experience to a videogame. And you did it. You nailed it. Good job!

item.title: Does AI need to be in EVERYTHING?
item.description: Billionaires want you to become so dependent on AI that they finally get what they really want -- complete control over your life.

item.title: Mailfaux: Fields of Faux
item.description: Our local Malifaux tournament was a chance to play against the best the game has to offer.

item.title: Backed the Vectrex Mini
item.description: Here's what I know about it. Which is a bit.

item.title: HeroQuest First Light #5: The Horrors of Synchroform
item.description: Halfway through the quests, we discover the secret of Qwindrak's transformation...

item.title: Quick Reviews
item.description: A little bit about games I've played recently, including Winter Burrow, Death by Scrolling, Alabaster Dawn, Solasta II, Mario Kart World and Pokemon Legends Z-A.

item.title: Gormenghast Book 1: Titus Groan
item.description: It's a book filled with fencing, fighting, torture, revenge, giants, monsters, chases, escapes, true love, and miracles!

item.title: DragonStrike: The Aerial Combat Board Game
item.description: With Amazon's "New World" MMO shuttering, can a simple board game provide a new home for its players?

item.title: Malifaux 4e: Rasputina vs Seamus
item.description: I showed this to my opponent. "Look, snipers, on a rooftop, in a book depository, shooting a VIP and his partner? No?" Nope. Meant nothing to him.

item.title: A Kolam for a Delivery Man
item.description: Waiting for a package that never arrives? Maybe all you need is a little practical magic...

item.title: It's the end of year, new console extravaganza
item.description: First comes the Switch 2, maybe someday the Analogue 3D and the Vectrex Mini?

item.title: Big Changes for Advent of Code 2025
item.description: Half the puzzles, no global leaderboard, and definitely... no AI.

item.title: Steam Next Fested
item.description: Steam suggested 30+ games to me in Next Fest that it thought I would enjoy. They think I enjoy survival crafting games, that much is clear.

item.title: Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles, a retrospective
item.description: Thoughts on beating the game for the fifth time across all its versions, from the original PlayStation, to the PSP, and now the modern remake.

item.title: Taking Control of my Skeets and Toots
item.description: I lost so much stuff when I left Twitter, and again when G+ shut down. Never again. I'm using WordLand to control my social media.

item.title: Malifaux: Lady J meets the Cult of December
item.description: I died to a FREAKIN' PINATA?

item.title: Falling out of love with Duolingo
item.description: The owl's tireless hunger for my money has finally pushed me out of the nest.

item.title: The Zen of Final Fantasy Tactics
item.description: There's the recommended way to play Final Fantasy Tactics. And then there's the way I play it.

item.title: Social media is a terrible way to find blogs to read
item.description: The answer comes from the forgotten history of blogging itself.

item.title: I cancelled my Pokémon TCG Pocket premium subscription
item.description: The latest expansion pack is really a slap in the face of dedicated collectors.

item.title: Malifaux 4e: Rasputina vs Maxine
item.description: "What do you mean, I can destroy your ice pillars?" The Arcanists face off against the Explorer Society in a match nobody saw coming.

item.title: Becoming Unreasonably Angry about Sonic Crossworlds Racing
item.description: The Fornite-ization of an iconic kart racing series has just gotten out of control.

item.title: Frosthaven #18 -- Crystal Fields
item.description: Back to Frosthaven with an entirely new threat -- and a weighty decision to make.

item.title: Dune Awakening: Apotheosis
item.description: I had a vision of what life in the Deep Desert might be like, and it happened? Also, if Funcom names an expansion "Apotheosis", they owe me money.

item.title: Dune Awakening: Life in the Deep Desert
item.description: It's not just worms and spice and dust and sandstorms and heat and... well, actually, that's pretty much what it is.

item.title: HeroQuest First Light: The Fortress of the Fire King
item.description: The Witch Queen Kessandria encounters the FO part of FAFO.

item.title: Retro World Expo 2025: A Guilty (Gear) Pleasure
item.description: I brought my Atari messenger bag with one game in it, and turned that into a bag full of games.

item.title: Frosthaven #114: Work Freeze
item.description: Sure, we'll place these explosives for you. They're safe, right guys? Guys? Where'd you all go?

item.title: Dune Awakening: Order of the Wombat Steps Out
item.description: I'm sad that this carrier's future is going to be as burning scrap next to a large spice blow as enemy assault 'thopters hover nearby.

item.title: Still More Confessions of a Kickstarter Superbacker
item.description: Oh dear. I keep backing Kickstarters. Why? WHY? I dunno. Anyway, here's what I'm waiting for today.

item.title: The Mystery of the Casterville Legion
item.description: @Nimgimli used AI Dungeon to make him an adventure. I wondered if I could get the same or better for free.

item.title: Sword Hero: Combat Arena Demo
item.description: This solo fantasy RPG asks the question, "What if combat was super dangerous?"

item.title: August Blogging from 2005 to today
item.description: It's been hours since Blaugust officially ended; is it too late to do a retrospective? How about for every August from the blog's start in 2005?

item.title: The Vectrex is coming back... in pog form!
item.description: Well, in mini form! It's true! Your chance to play Vectrex games as if you were twice as large as you are now is coming soon.

item.title: Dune Awakening: We finally got ganked
item.description: Dune: Awakening is a PvP game, and our base was in a PvP area, so it wasn't entirely unexpected...

item.title: I guess I suck at this whole Blaugust thing
item.description: I had such plans, but in the end, I didn't do so well.

item.title: Frosthaven Scenario #10: Crystal Enclosure
item.description: Where we run afoul of a Drinking Donkey and evil Ice Pillars.

item.title: Herdling
item.description: Care for and guide a growing herd of cattle-like Calicorn to a mysterious destination in this "Journey"-like wordless game of wonder and danger.

item.title: The Kind of Malifaux You Play at Home
item.description: With 4th Edition leaving beta, players are waiting for the official release. Meanwhile, some of us still want to play a game...

item.title: Downsizing!
item.description: Can I replace an aging desktop computer with a Raspberry Pi the size of a deck of cards?

item.title: Dune: Awakening -- Roc Music
item.description: After a bunch of crafting and farming, Team Spode builds the largest ornithopter in Dune: Awakening. Now what?

item.title: Base44: Games from a Prompt
item.description: Yes, I'm going to talk about AI-created games here. Feel free to go off on me IN THE COMMENTS :-)

item.title: Micro Hero: Hercules
item.description: Hercules has to complete a dozen tough jobs to atone for killing his family. Only you can help him in this solo deckbuilding game!

item.title: Frosthaven Scenario #5: Frozen Crypt
item.description: Our heroes head back to the mainland to take on a race against time...

item.title: Lilliput's Workshop
item.description: Do you like little wooden train sets and solving puzzles? Well, if you do, have I got a game for you...

item.title: Soda can tabs for charity? REALLY?
item.description: Feels like a scam, but apparently this is real. Stupid, but real.

item.title: La Brute: Battles Royale and Warring Guilds
item.description: Beneath this seeming auto battler is a surprisingly deep strategy game...

item.title: International Play Your Vectrex Day is November 1!
item.description: The world comes together for just one day each year to enjoy a forgotten 1980s home arcade classic -- the GCE Vectrex. Also, a new Vectrex song.

item.title: I Know What I Did Last Weekend
item.description: My monthly grab bag of stuff I've been up to recently...

item.description: It's hot troll-on-unicorn action as Karrock and Pryzm take on the plague of corruption infecting their world.

item.description: Is Dune: Awakening even an MMO if it doesn't have fishing? My playgroup moves into the Deep Desert and finds...

item.title: The pandemic wasn't all bad, actually...
item.description: Not to minimize the people who got sick or lost family members, but being confined for a few months had its perks.

item.title: Blaugust kommt.
item.description: I didn't think Blaugust 2025 would happen, but then the community stepped up. Also, reflections on using (and NOT using) AI art in blogs.

item.title: Frosthaven Scenario 14: Jagged Shoals
item.description: They say that a pig can consume a body in eight minutes. What about ten pigs? That are also fish somehow?

item.title: HeroQuest First Light: Second Adventure
item.description: The one where an elf dies and a wizard retires and we meet someone surprising.

item.title: Malifaux: Boilermaker takes the stage
item.description: For Canada Day, a special crew. Not all of the Foundry crew are from Canada, but some are, and they have a proud history of bending molten metal to their will.

item.title: Palworld Does What Nintendon't. Again.
item.description: Palworld's new update does more than let your Pals become their true selves. There's also fishing. And, almost forgot, a portal to the world of Terraria!

item.title: Dune Awakening: Walking Without Rhythm
item.description: Also: walking without WATER. Also: Why isn't there a "walk without rhythm" button? Also: What if I just have, like, natural rhythm? What do I do then?

item.title: Frosthaven #7: The Edge of the World
item.description: Taking a break from the Algox situation to have a seafood snack.

item.title: HeroQuest: First Light: First Adventure
item.description: After a couple of years, a Target HeroQuest exclusive edition brings the game back to the table.

item.title: The Dishonesty of Balatro
item.description: Now that I have finally won the game, I have some thoughts...

item.title: Dark Quest 4: More Hero Quest than ever?
item.description: Can Dark Quest 4 satisfy your insatiable appetite for Hero Quest, except on a computer?

item.title: Frosthaven: Heart of Ice (Scenario 4)
item.description: Took us long enough, but we finally had another Frosthaven night. Spoilers!

item.title: Malifaux 4e: December Meets the Family
item.description: Or, Perdita, Neverborn Hunter, runs head-first into the Wall of Ice at the Edge of the World.

item.description: Sometimes I just want to play games to relax. Sometimes.

item.description: He's found the saucer news. Don't report this. The end of Path of Fire was a thing that happened.

item.description: It's about mounts. In Guild Wars 2.

item.title: Malifaux 4e: December vs Experimental
item.description: For just my second 4th edition game, my brave Rasputina (Abominable) faced the evil Dr. McMourning (Insanitary). We were at a fight and a hockey game broke out.

item.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 1/3
item.description: Clair Obscure: great gameplay, characters and milieu, but its third act seems in a hurry to get off the stage.

item.title: PAX East 2025: Part Deux
item.description: Traipsing through Boston rain didn't do anything for my cold, so my PAX weekend ended early. Still, here's more of what we saw there.

item.title: PAX East 2025: Wow, kinda big, right?
item.description: First time at PAX, having a great time, wish you were here.

item.title: Blue Prince: Welcome to the Gift Shop
item.description: Hey, I completed another game! And what a journey...

item.title: How Kickstarter Projects are Handling Tariffs
item.description: I might have a Kickstarter problem. I went to all the campaign pages for the games I haven't yet received to find out how tariffs were affecting them.

item.title: Malifaux 4th Edition: Mei Feng vs Captain Zipp
item.description: Foundry and Infamous meet once again... and the result is pretty much same as last time.

item.title: Frosthaven Scenario #1: Town in Flames
item.description: Having completed the tutorial scenario and let a month pass before schedules matched up again, we finally started Frosthaven for reals.

item.title: Malifaux: Preparing for 4th Edition
item.description: I've spent the last couple of weeks trying to get a mostly new crew ready for 4th edition...

item.title: I Know What I Did Last Weekend (April edition)
item.description: Blue Prince, Monster Hunter Wilds, Sword for Hire news, some new boardgames, something new coming for Malifaux, and probably more...

item.title: Tariffs will destroy the board game industry, and I am there for it.
item.description: I think we have to look on the bright side of the death of Kickstarted board games.

item.title: Game Night: Frosthaven! And So It Begins...
item.description: After a few years on the shelf, we're finally playing Frosthaven. It's pretty different from Gloomhaven; could be a real challenge.

item.title: I Know What I Did Last Weekend
item.description: Arcade Paradise, A Dark Room, Pokemon TCG Pocket, Frosthaven and an update on Sword for Hire. LOTS O' STUFF.

item.title: Monster Hunters Wilding
item.description: I like to think of the Monster Hunter series being set in the same world as the Pokemon games.

item.title: Game Night: Bardwood Grove
item.description: Run around a forest, do bard stuff, become the most famous bard in Bardwood Grove.

item.title: Malifaux: New Crew Two
item.description: Rasputina meets the alien, mind-controlling Cadmus project on the streets of Malifaux City.

item.title: Sword for Hire: Vertical Slice & Vibe Coding
item.description: When it's mapped out, Sword for Hire isn't as large as it looks in the book. But, it is possible to play through the game and level up.

item.title: Game Night: CULTivate
item.description: This isn't a picture of the CULT-ivate board game. It's my cat, Midnight, helping me to play "The Plucky Squire". He was really getting into it.

item.title: Malifaux: "New Crew" League, Week 1
item.description: The local gaming store is running a league this spring for new players or for players who want to play a crew they've never played before.

item.description: Also, lots of Tunnels & Trolls-adjacent stuff.

item.title: "YES", the first cheat code.

item.description: I may be committing some light copyright infringement...

item.title: First Impressions: Stars Reach
item.description: The game is very much in development and far from finished, but I think I see where it wants to go.

item.title: Steam Next Fest: Day 1, the Weird Stuff
item.description: It's the day when indies strut their stuff! Quick looks at Infinity Cats Project, Desktop Survivors 98, Inconceivable Rat Endeavor and Make Your Choice.

item.title: Canvas: Game Night has been Framed
item.description: A timeless piece of art or a directionless collage of imagery that belongs on a refrigerator door?

item.title: The Great Wave Off Kanagawa, by Hokusai
item.description: Reproducing this woodblock print with 3D printing gives me even more appreciation for the original work.

item.title: Hueforge: High Rez Color Pictures on your 3D Printer
item.description: Hueforge prints using very thin layers so that colors bleed through to create blends, shades and gradients on any printer.

item.title: Wot's... Uh the Deal?
item.description: Lots of stuff going on, I guess I should update on all the things.

item.title: Erenshor: Oh, by the way, which one is Bink?
item.description: Erenshor is the single player RPG you tell MMO stories about.

item.title: Rolling Dice and Taking Names: My CaptainCon 2025 Experience
item.description: We traveled through the Quiet Corner to the exotic Rhode Island to chew bubblegum and play board games. There was a lot more going on than just Malifaux!

item.title: Malifaux: Booty & Plunder
item.description: Rasputina and her crew are painted and ready for "primed" time in her first serious match-up.

item.title: I Know What I Did Last Weekend
item.description: Some updates on games and game-related dinguses from this past weekend...

item.title: AI is going to steal my job -- and I couldn't be happier
item.description: Taking an idea to reality takes just minutes, even if you don't know one thing about programming. It's a new world.

item.title: FANTASIAN Neo Dimension (Part 1)
item.description: A throwback to the classic JRPGs set in handcrafted dioramas, Fantasian Neo Dimension is a love letter to the Final Fantasy series.

item.title: Malifaux: The Littlest Cap'n
item.description: The local Malifaux club decided to hold a practice tournament ahead of Captain Con at the end of the month. I brought my new crew to give them a baptism by ice.

item.title: Our STAR*VADERS!
item.description: It's "Slay the Spire" meets "Into the Breach" meets "Space Invaders" meets... Space Battleship Yamato?

item.title: I Know What I Did Last Weekend
item.description: This weekend was a little time in a lot of games. There's a game or two in here you might not have heard of.

item.title: Best Board Games of 2024!
item.description: The last Best Of post! We play a lot of board games. Stems from our crippling Kickstarter habit. Step into my tabletop world...

item.title: Best of 2024: My Favorite MMOs
item.description: I played a few MMOs this past year. Here's some of my favorites, including two from way back when.

item.title: The Best Strategy/Tactics RPGs of 2024
item.description: Tactics RPGs have been a favorite of mine since Final Fantasy Tactics. These are the best strategy/tactics RPGs I played in 2024.

item.title: Best of 2024: Survival Crafting Games
item.description: If you've ever punched a tree, you've played a survival crafting game. There were a lot of these in 2024. Here's the ones I played.

item.title: Best of 2024: Single-Player RPGs
item.description: 2024 was a great year for RPGs. I didn't play all the ones I wanted, but the ones I did play were pretty incredible.

item.title: The Best of 2024: Adventure Games
item.description: They were a mainstay of 80s gaming and then all but disappeared until Portal revolutionized the genre. They are still some of the most fun you can have on a computer.

item.title: The Best Puzzle Games of 2024
item.description: These were the games that made me think this year.

item.title: The Best Simulation/City Builder Games of 2024
item.description: These are the simulation / city builder games I have played during 2024. It's not a comprehensive list! Some gems in here, though.

item.title: Best of 2024: Arcade Games
item.description: I played dozens of games in 2024. Trying to distill them down to "the best" is hard. First up: arcade games.

item.title: Billionaire Rapture
item.description: Sometimes I just want to write a story. Art AI, words are all mine, for better or worse. I hope you enjoy it.

item.title: Invading the Ship of Heroes
item.description: The superheroes-in-space MMO will release in the second quarter of 2025. Today, they let us play one of the first raids...

item.title: Advent of Code 2024: The First Week
item.description: AI has changed programming forever. It has changed Advent of Code and the way I work. What's life going to be like when AI can do my job, better than me?

item.title: Bonaparte: A Mechanized Revolution
item.description: Take one French Revolution, subtract Napoleon, add a couple relatives you never heard about, and toss in a few giant mechs...

item.title: Malifaux Night: Literally Hell
item.description: Tuesday night Malifaux. The snow outside was no match for the ice that waited for me inside.

item.title: Advent of Code 2024.1:
item.description: Where is that darned elvish historian?

item.title: Star Wars: Outlaws. Just you and your "cat" against the galaxy.
item.description: This game took a lot of flack for being a buggy mess when it came out, but at least on the PS5, it's a fun, if slow paced, open world adventure in the Star Wars universe.

item.title: Odin Sphere: Two dimensions is more than enough
item.description: It's a 2D side-scrolling action RPG about a brave Valkyrie and her overbearing father, Odin.

item.title: Spy Muppets: License to Croak
item.description: The credits are longer than the game in this classic Gameboy Advance caper...

item.title: LEGO Horizon Adventures
item.description: I hurried through Dragon Age: the Veilguard because I knew this was waiting for me to finish.

item.title: Dragon Age: the Veilguard finished
item.description: 82 hours from start to end, all quests completed, all companions at max bond, all factions but one maxed out, 47/52 achievements. Was it worth it?

item.title: Malifaux: Pig Farm
item.description: Pig Love was the name of the game in last night’s exciting match against the infamous Captain Zipp.

item.title: Dragon Age: Saboteur
item.description: Can I win Dragon Age: Veilguard with a bow that has just one arrow and takes ten seconds to reload?

item.title: Dragon Age: The Veilguard is not the game you think it is
item.description: But maybe the Dragon Age series has always been like this...

item.title: Malifaux: A Close Race
item.description: I thought I would stave off the existential dread of the election by playing Malifaux... little did I know...

item.title: Game Night: Tales from the Loop
item.description: Goonies meets Stranger Things meets robots in a board game set in an alternate 80s Sweden, inspired by the art of Simon Stålenhag.

item.title: Malifaux-ing in Massachusetts
item.description: To celebrate Halloween, Kasul and I traveled to Webways in Marlborough, Massachusetts for a Carver Day tournament. Carver Day?

item.title: Greek Gods Exist and they are Living Among Us!
item.description: "Stray Gods: The Roleplaying Musical" puts a Broadway spin on the problems of immortal gods...

item.title: Beyond Shadowgate: The SECRET Menu?!
item.description: If you're a Beyond Shadowgate speedrunner, or are considering a promising career in Shadowgate speedrunning, you're going to want to read this.

item.title: Last Call BBS: Puzzle Games for the Retro-Inclined
item.description: The last game from the makers of TIS-100, SpaceChem, Exapunks and many other puzzle games feels like a Greatest Hits collection.

item.title: Game Night: In Too Deep, Terraforming Mars, what comes next?
item.description: Catching up on game night posts. We played with the super duper "all in" Terraforming Mars gear and finished up In Too Deep. What's next for Game Night?

item.title: EverCraft Online: The Unholy Child of EQ and MineCraft
item.description: Not a MineCraft mod, ECO brings back the 1999 experience in style -- block style.

item.title: Malifaux: "Fall into Faux" tournament
item.description: This tournament was the culmination of many games and a lot of paint.

item.title: Analogue 3D: If I were to get one...
item.description: Do I love Nintendo 64 games enough to buy a $250 console that would bring them into the 21st century?

item.title: Erenshor: The Shivering Steps
item.description: Took me a minute and some leading hints but I finally worked my way through the Halloween content and killed all six bosses.

item.title: October retro finds!
item.description: We didn’t find much in our latest dive into the local retro game store scene, but we’d found a couple things. Mostly.

item.title: The Lord of the Rings: War in the North
item.description: Snowblind Studios' swan song might have been the best RPG they ever made.

item.title: Justice League Heroes: Worth eBaying for?
item.description: Loved Champions of Norrath and Return to Arms. Snowblind's next game was Justice League Heroes. Can it be just as good? Better?

item.title: Those Who Rule: new demo!
item.description: Those crazy kids and their ruling! Story driven tactical RPG that punches well above its weight, you wouldn't know this was the work of a single developer.

item.title: LEGO Thunderjaw: Finally mostly complete!
item.description: Don't look at it too hard, pieces will start falling off. In fact, don't even look at this PICTURE too long. Putting this together was a leeeeetle bit stressful.

item.title: Champions: Return to Arms finished!
item.description: Hey, I finished another game! I hope this doesn't become some kind of trend. I had a lot of fun with it.

item.title: Game Night: In Too Deep
item.description: You're a psychic cop hijacking the minds of supervillains to solve crimes. But mind control... it's a two way street.

item.title: Malifaux: The Scrappy Underdog
item.description: It was my final league game ahead of the tournament. I've learned a few lessons that may help me the day of.

item.title: Beyond Shadowgate -- how to get endings A, B, C and D.
item.description: I couldn't leave Beyond Shadowgate behind unless I'd managed to get all four endings. Here's how.

item.title: Beyond Shadowgate: Hey, I actually finished a game!
item.description: Thanks to a lot of help from chat! Am... am I a streamer now?

item.title: Beyond Shadowgate: a classic reimagined.
item.description: It's the 80s again! The developers of the original Shadowgate are back 35 years later with a sequel that doubles down on everything that made the first game great.

item.title: This Halloween, Erenshor Gets Spooky!
item.description: I can see this becoming a good "I got half an hour, I'll just do this dungeon" game.

item.title: Upping my game with AI
item.description: Designing better game streams with AI feedback—turning creative ideas into reality.

item.title: The Utter Insanity of Nemesis
item.description: Survivors face escalating dangers aboard a doomed starship in the final game of Nemesis.

item.title: Weekend gaming: Janthir Wilds, Return to Arms, more Thunderjaw
item.description: I finish Guild Wars 2’s Janthir Wilds prologue, revisit **Champions: Return to Arms** with a PS4 controller, and make progress on my LEGO Thunderjaw build.

item.title: The End of the Dragon of Icespire Peak
item.description: Our party takes on the white dragon, Cryovein, in a tense fortress battle, but the victory feels surprisingly unremarkable.

item.title: Fall into Faux: The One Where I Win
item.description: Mei Feng’s Foundry crew faces off against Molly’s undead in a battle for ballot boxes, with unexpected twists and tactical plays. Can I turn my crew’s mobility into victory?

item.title: The Tale of Tiffany, Lord of the Ring
item.description: Story and picture were generated by AI. Prompts by me. What if a modern teenager isekai'd herself into the Lord of the Rings?

item.title: The Transgressive Charm of Um Jammer Lammy
item.description: All "Jammer" Lammy wants to do is rock. The only thing stopping her is... everything.

item.title: My California: Lick Observatory
item.description: What was once the largest refracting telescope in the world looks out high above Silicon Valley…

item.title: Blaugust 2024 is in the bag!
item.description: It's always a struggle to make it to the finish line, but I also always have fun.

item.title: My California
item.description: If you live on the Central Coast, you already know.

item.title: Going to California
item.description: I took a chance on a big jet plane, don’t let anyone tell you that they’re all the same.

item.title: Erenshor -- EverQuest for soloers
item.description: It's not an MMO, it's an MMO simulator where you can handpick the party of your dreams and clear dungeons, kill bosses, and find uber lewtz.

item.title: Twitch Drops: Why?
item.description: It's an army of low-effort streamers playing commercials to hundreds of hidden browser tabs.

item.title: Building Thunderjaw — Day 1
item.description: My big birthday present this year was a LEGO Thunderjaw to go along with my LEGO Tallneck. It’s time to start putting it together.

item.title: Retro World Expo 2024: Adventure Awaits
item.description: That's another Retro World Expo under our belts, and we got Some Stuff. But I could have spent thousands more...

item.title: Nexus: Kingdom of the Wind
item.description: Most people have never heard of NexusTK. Some people maybe heard of it long ago. It's different for me. Nexus: Kingdom of the Wind was my only MMO before EverQuest rewrote the MMO rulebook.

item.title: EverQuest: Five Epic Tales
item.description: There was a discussion on Mastodon yesterday (thanks, Nimgimli!) about the stories we tell about our MMO characters. Here's five EverQuest stories about mine.

item.title: PS2 RPGs: Quick Reviews
item.description: You're down at K-Mart or Sears, looking at all those shiny new PlayStation 2 games in the sharp glass case; which one is the lady behind the counter going to bring out for you?

item.title: My short, brief history with Crocs
item.description: My son refuses to be seen with me while wearing them. It's like wearing a scarlet letter. But they are soooo cool.

item.title: Malifaux: League champion?
item.description: Building on Saturday's Malifaux tournament, yesterday was the start of the 2024 Fall Malifaux league and I came the closest yet to actually winning a match.

item.title: Weekend Gaming: Ship of Heroes, Persona 5 Tactica, Giants Citizen Kabuto
item.description: And also that entire day playing Malifaux...

item.title: My first Malifaux tournament!
item.description: I'ma start by explaining what the game is, as best I can anyway.

item.title: La Brute: You should totally join my clan
item.description: This game was super popular 15 years ago but you should play it now.

item.title: Could this be the end of Palworld?
item.description: For me, I mean. Could this be the end of Palworld -- for me. Betteridge's law of headlines may apply.

item.title: Champions: Return to Arms
item.description: In Champions of Norrath, you're tasked with single-handedly killing the God of Hate. Now they want you to kill the God of War in the sequel. Firiona and her crew are really pretty useless, aren't they?

item.title: Painting the White Dragon
item.description: I printed the White Dragon in white filament. Then I told the D&D group that I still needed to paint it. "Why? It's already white?" Ha!

item.title: Breakhack: Fight, Die, Repeat
item.description: A dev decided one day to gather freely given assets and combine that with some 'C' code and make a really cool roguelike. Just the thing to waste a few minutes now and then.

item.title: D&D: I -- no, WE -- have a white dragon problem
item.description: The problem is, we're about to battle the final boss battle of the Icespire campaign, but Cryovain refuses to come out of his dressing room.

item.title: Worshippers of Cthulhu: the Demo
item.description: Loosing an ultimate evil upon the world starts with clean clothing, good food, well-built homes and safe streets.

item.title: AI has transformed the way I code
item.description: Github Copilot and other LLM-powered coding tools have become indispensable to many in the software development industry. AI is not a fad; it's here to stay.

item.title: It's my birthday, but *I* get the presents!
item.description: My boyfriend knows exactly the kind of stuff I love, but you'd expect that. Read on for why all this stuff is SO FREAKIN' COOL.

item.title: Did a robot write this?
item.description: When it was revealed that more than one blog was relying upon AI-generated content, the blogosphere erupted in righteous indignation. Why?

item.title: How to lose $2K without hardly trying
item.description: Talking about my triumphant crypto journey and the joy of HODL.

item.title: Game Night: The Penultimate Nemesis
item.description: It was a typical mission until the walls of our starship started whispering words of madness to us...

item.title: Not Gonna Lose My Blog: Backups Saved Me Once and Will Save Me Again
item.description: I lost my blog. Backups saved me. Now I'm taking it to the next level by implementing the 3-2-1 backup strategy with rclone and OneDrive.

item.title: Ultimate Victory: Dominating Palworld’s Challenging Endgame Raids
item.description: To take on this boss, you need to be ferocious, serene, muscle-headed and impatient.

item.title: More Confessions of a Kickstarter Superbacker
item.description: It's like the number one sign you have too much money: logging into Kickstarter. Here's an update on what games are coming my way and (hopefully) onto the table.

item.title: Legends of Kesmai: The first MMORPG
item.description: I love old school, retro games. Maybe not quite THAT retro, though...

item.title: Welcome to Blaugust, 2024!
item.description: Shout out to Belghast for all of his incredibly hard and professional work to bring all these people together to celebrate the lost art of blogging! We don't deserve him.

item.title: Dungeon Crawl Classics TTRPG: Survival of the Fittest
item.description: Why play one character when you could play five? DCC is a table top RPG with a different perspective on roleplaying.

item.title: Bienvenue à monsieur Minuit, mon nouveau chat noir
item.description: Well, that's about all the French I know...

item.title: Blaugust 2024 and DC Heroes United
item.description: Sure, I could use two posts to write about two things... OR I could do this, and write about them both. It's a Value Size Blog.

item.title: Backlog Report: Is it worth buying a PS3 to play Mad Maestro?
item.description: Mad Maestro is a PS2 classical music rhythm game. Why would you need a PS3 to play it?

item.title: Backlog Report: Star Trek Conquest
item.description: I promised myself I would work through my PS2 game backlog by playing every game at least a couple hours. First up: Star Trek Conquest.

item.title: The White Raven: A Cinematic Tactical RPG
item.description: After two years, GatherRound Games has released their first peek at their upcoming Fire Emblem-style tactical game.

item.title: Nemesis: The Board Game
item.description: Stop me if you've heard this one before. You've been awakened from hibernation by your starship's AI. Something has gone wrong. It's... Aliens. I mean Intruders.

item.title: Origins 2024 Recap
item.description: This was my first Origins. It was a little overwhelming, but I'll do better, next time.

item.title: It's not writer's block, it's distraction
item.description: I wanted to write about Origins 2024, but then I just got to wondering if I could write an aquarium simulator for an ASCII terminal, and eventually this happened.

item.title: Valheim: The Endless Corpse Run
item.description: Getting ready for killing the Goblin King was bad enough. Mistlands is worse.

item.title: HeroQuest: Return to Barak-Tor
item.description: It's the final quest of the starter campaign. How was it? Is this game even worth playing in 2024?

item.title: SacriFire: A love letter to the golden age of JRPGS
Retro Haul: Tactics and Sea Monkeys
item.description: We found ourselves once again awash in a sea of retro games. We found some winners, maybe...?

item.title: Valheim: Ashenlands Bound
item.description: I didn't think I'd be playing Valheim again, but here we are. Let's try something different, this time.

item.title: Retro Game Haul: Tron Deadly Discs and Reversi
item.description: The sale of the Intellivision brand to the Atari brand means it's finally legal for me to play Intellivision games after only 40 years.

item.title: George Plimpton Has Risen from His Grave
item.description: The dregs of Atari has bought out the remains of Intellivision, the seventh seal is broken, and chaos has descended upon a burning world.

item.title: HeroQuest: Quest for the Spirit Blade
item.description: Last time, we accidentally woke the Witch Lord. Oops. Now we are in search of the one weapon that can put him down.

item.title: Midjourney goes Web
item.description: Midjourney has famously relied upon the Discord app to be the front end to its image generation AI. But now, all you need is a browser and a prompt.

item.title: HeroQuest: Barak Tor - The Barrow of the Witch Lord
item.description: We're nearing the end of the first HeroQuest campaign... and we're up against an undefeatable lich. Should be fun.

item.title: ADVENT: Colossal Cave 3D
item.description: Having spent the past several days mapping and exploring the text version, it's finally time to replay Colossal Cave Adventure in glorious 3D.

item.title: ADVENT: Seasoned Adventurer
item.description: I got all the treasures I knew about... now, how about the treasures I don't know about?

item.title: ADVENT: Twisty Passages, All Alike
item.description: A pirate stole our treasure! Time to steal it BACK. But first, we have to find our way through one of the most notorious mazes of all time...

item.title: ADVENT: The Secrets of Adventure
item.description: What you need to know before you begin to play Crowther and Woods' "Colossal Cave Adventure".

item.title: ADVENT: The Road to Adventure
item.description: You can draw a direct line from Crowther and Woods' "Adventure" to MMOs such as EverQuest. I'm going to be playing it all the way through... twice.

item.title: EverQuest II goes retro with Anashti Sul
item.description: It's probably intentional that the latest EverQuest 2 server, Anashti Sul, is named after a deity once known as the "Lifegiver", but now more known for bringing life to the dead.

item.title: PCSX2 has gone and done it this time...
item.description: The latest updates to the PCSX2 PlayStation 2 emulator have done amazing things for its convenience and compatibility.

item.title: The Nightmare of Druaga (PS2)
item.description: It's a roguelike, it's the next chapter in TWO long running game series... but is it any good?

item.title: Manor Lords might be okay, actually
item.description: City builder sims usually are so addictive that often avoid them, but I couldn't resist this one; it has the one feature I always wanted from this type of game.

item.title: Beyond Shadowgate -- the Demo
item.description: Still trying to figure out the puzzles in that old NES cart of Shadowgate you still somehow have? Well, you can finally take the game out of your NES because the sequel is finally here, almost.

item.title: Confessions of a Kickstarter Superbacker
item.description: I haven't talked about my recent kickstarter projects, so here's a bunch of board games, some video games, and some other stuff.

item.title: That is not a sandworm in your driveway
item.description: Just random stuff I've asked ChatGPT...

item.title: Palworld @ 100%. Finally.
item.description: Palworld, Palworld... I wish I knew how to quit you. Also: PvP?

item.title: Is Terraforming Mars... too good?
item.description: Terraforming Mars has been on the list of top 10 board games since first published in 2016. It's good, but is it TOO good?

item.title: Palworld's First Raid is a Killer
item.description: I was gonna go with something clickbaity like, "can you beat the legendary Palworld level 50 raid boss with only a stick and level 1 pals?". I bet someone out there CAN, but not me.

item.title: Retro Game Haul: March 30, 2024
item.description: I have way too many games to ever play, but still I can't help it. Today's haul: Othello (NES), Spinball (Vectrex), Colossal Cave Adventure (PS5), Broken Sword: The Shadow of the Templars (GBA). Read on for more details...

item.title: The Atari 400 Mini. Is it worth the nostalgia?
item.description: Betteridge's law of headlines is in play here, but... The Atari 800, the 400's big brother, was my first personal computer, and I have a lot of fond memories teaching myself to program on it.

item.title: Gloomhaven: Buttons & Bugs
item.description: What if Gloomhaven, but tiny? Cephalofair has managed to cram 10kg of board game into a box no bigger than four decks of cards stacked together.

item.title: Starship Simulator: The Demo
item.description: About 200 lightyears inward from Sol hangs a giant nebula in the middle of nothing, and you can go there.

item.title: HeroQuest: Murder, Incorporated.
item.description: Okay, the actual title of the quest is "Bastion of Chaos", but that's not how it felt to us. We're the murderers in the title, btw.

item.title: Dragon's Dogma II: Now Playing
item.description: I really, genuinely need to blog more promptly about the games I'm playing. Unicorn Overlord is done; I'm drowning in the world of Dragon's Dogma II now.

item.title: Unicorn Overlord -- Whose Overlord Is It, Anyway?
item.description: After playing through this amazing real-time tactics RPG, the only question I hadn't answered was... were we the baddies all the time?

item.title: HeroQuest: The Castle of Mystery
item.description: This teleporting puzzle box separated the heroes across a deadly dungeon. How many would survive to escape?

item.title: Retro Game Quick Look: Sword of Mana, LotR: Two Towers, Iridion 3D
item.description: I shuffled through the Gameboy Advance discount bins at some local retro game stores, and this is what I came away with.

item.title: Palworld is a thing that happened, I guess
item.description: I'm still playing Palworld, but I'm really not sure why...

item.title: HeroQuest: A Race Against Time
item.description: Spoiler alert: There was no time limit for this quest.

item.title: Palworld, Vandal Hearts, Hons VR Lenses
item.description: Okay, there's a lot of gaming stuff going on so I'm just going to go over some of it, briefly.

item.title: Vectrex: The Console You Really Wanted
item.description: I didn't want to blog about my Vectrex just yet, but I'm just way to squeee at the moment, so...

item.title: HeroQuest: The Fire Mage
item.description: The monk and the rogue take the place of the barbarian and the wizard as the party faces their greatest challenge yet...

item.title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink (DEMO)
item.description: Granblue Fantasy makes the leap from mobile to console with Relink. So... what kind of game is it?

item.title: City of Heroes: Homecoming...
item.description: It's probably been 15 years since I last played City of Heroes... and now it's come back. Should I?

item.title: HeroQuest: The Monks and the Rogues step up
item.description: Barbarian, Dwarf, Elf and Wizard are looking around nervously...

item.title: Pokemon Violet: Mochi Mayhem
item.description: Finished with Pokemon Scarlet/Violet... again... with a strange little episode about a Pokemon who forces people to do the chicken dance?

item.title: HeroQuest: The Stone Hunter
item.description: Taking the dwarf from solving crossword puzzles to kicking skellie butt.

item.title: Trash Goblin Demo
item.description: One goblin's trash is another goblin's treasure...

item.title: LEGO Tallneck + Lightailing lighting kit
item.description: I really wanted a LEGO Tallneck, all lit up, so I bought one, built it, and lit it up.

item.title: Tactics Ogre: The Death of Shaher
item.description: Just some AI art inspired by Shaher's death and Eleanor's sacrifice in Tactics Ogre: The Knight of Lodis.

item.title: Tactics Ogre: The Knight of Lodis FINISHED!
item.description: It's a tale of love, loss, betrayal, alliances, war, peace, redemption, and a young knight's journey to discover his place in the world.

item.title: Christmas Loot: Knight of Lodis and Prince of Zenobia
item.description: For Christmas, I got two more entries in the Tactics Ogre/Ogre Battle series... one I have played before, and the other is going to need some work to be able to play... and understand.

item.title: Goodbye, Lannister
item.description: The story of my chocolate Maine Coon, Lannister, who died today.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 22 -- Sand Slabs
item.description: I almost gave up on this one. Then I started playing around with it in a 3D render package and I found the issue.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 21 -- Step Counter
item.description: I had 80% of the solution, but that last 20% killed it. This is the day I failed Advent of Code 2023.

item.title: Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen (alpha peek)
item.description: More Vanguard than EverQuest, Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen tries to strike a balance between old and new.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 20 -- Pulse Propagation
item.description: First, you find the right answer using pencil and paper. Then, you write code to produce that answer. Right? No? Just me?

item.title: Advent of Code Day 19 -- Aplenty
item.description: First puzzle in a few days where I didn't need to get hints. I guess the problem was musical, aerodynamic, shiny AND extremely cool looking!

item.title: Advent of Code Day 18 -- Lavaduct Lagoon
Advent of Code Day 17 -- Clumsy Crucible
item.description: Edsger Dijkstra is laughing at me from his grave.

item.title: D&D Session 3: Butterskull Ranch
item.description: The party heads to Butterskull Ranch in search of skulls. Seems reasonable.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 16 -- The Floor Will Be Lava
item.description: /u/YellowZorro hasn't posted any more art, so back to Dall-E 3 renderings. Today, we get ready to MAKE SOME LAVA TO SAVE CHRISTMAS!

item.title: Advent of Code Day 15 -- Lens Library
item.description: The lack of hands is all that is keeping the world's reindeer from lasering everyone to death. Luckily, THIS reindeer has YOU. Beware.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 14 -- Parabolic Reflector Dish
item.description: We're almost to the mountaintop where everything begins... but first we need to start a little fire.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 13 -- Point of Incidence
item.description: I think if I went to Iceland and stood on one of those lava fields, I would be convinced I was in another world.

item.title: HeroQuest: Prince Magnus' Gold
item.description: How can a single pit trap wipe out the entire party???

item.title: Advent of Code Day 12 -- Hot Springs
item.description: I narrowly avoided yesterday's trap only to walk right into today's.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 11 -- Cosmic Expansion
item.description: This is why Santa shouldn't let elves do astrophysics.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 10 -- Pipe Maze
item.description: Anyone else notice that this is the second puzzle in a row without any elves?

item.title: Monsters & Memories: Wish you were here?
item.description: Got a notice on Discord that Monsters & Memories was open for testing again, so in I went.

item.title: Advent of Code Days 8 and 9 -- Ghost Story
item.description: It took me so long to figure out the Day 8 puzzle that I didn't write it up before bed, so twofer today to catch up.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 7 -- Camel Cards
item.description: I really tried with Haskell this time. Oh well, looks like we'll have to play Camel Poker in Python instead.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 6 -- Wait For It
item.description: This one was easy. TOO easy. Day 7 is going to be bad.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 5 -- If You Give A Seed A Fertilizer
item.description: This can take no time at all or an eternity.

item.title: The Hobbit (Gameboy Advance)
item.description: See, it's a story about a little man who lives in a hole dug into a hill...

item.title: Advent of Code Day 4 -- Scratchcards
item.description: So, I'm back to solving the puzzle in Haskell again. This puzzle was easy enough for me to figure out.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 3 -- Gear Ratios
item.description: My Haskell experiments end. Even though I got the answer, have I failed?

item.title: Advent of Code Day 2 -- Cube Conundrum
item.description: The hands of the Christmas elf are far, far larger than they appear.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 1 -- Trebuchet?!
item.description: Elves can't fly, but maybe YOU can... with a little help from a friendly Christmas trebuchet...

item.title: Advent of Code 2023 -- Day 0
item.description: Oh heck... it's just about Advent of Code time! I am SO FAR from being ready.

item.description: Being the third foray into the dungeon. Sir Ragnar seeks revenge and only our blades and his gold can supply it.

item.description: This time, I saved them what needed saving.

item.description: The snarky computer guiding you through Superliminal might not actually be Portal's GLaDOS. but they at least went to the same school.

item.description: We need to have a little talk about Pantheon...

item.description: Webrings were like... guilds, but for blogs.

item.title: GW2: Unmounted Asura ISO Skyscale
item.description: All my friends have skyscales and I am jealous and also lazy.

item.title: D&D Session 2: Gnomengarde
item.description: Our adventuring party -- half-orc cleric Harka, barbarian Gregory, warlock Loreflower and the tricky bard with many banes -- travel to the tunnel city of rock gnomes, Gnomengarde.

item.title: Baldur's Gate 3: Guilty
item.description: I said I wasn't going to replay Baldur's Gate 3, but then I went ahead and did. Am doing.

item.description: We played the modern reprinting of the classic adventure game, HeroQuest. Even the first adventure may have been too much for our brave crew.

item.description: Sometimes, the silliest rules in Dungeons & Dragons can be a blessing in disguise...

item.description: Finally, after fifty years, housing comes to Dungeons & Dragons. Innkeepers across the land protest.

item.description: I played Baldur's Gate 3 while my partner played Starfield. Which of us had the most fun?

item.title: Baldur's Gate 3: Back on Track
item.description: There's always another way to do something in Baldur's Gate 3...

item.title: Baldur's Gate: It's been a long Act 2
item.description: I always do this. I get so wrapped up in a game that I forget to write about it, and when I try to write about it, there's too much!

item.title: Guild Wars 2: Dragon in Waiting
item.description: After a decade or so of playing Guild Wars 2, I'm finally working on my character's personal quest -- to save the world, and then rule it. Tonight: Chapter 6, "Retribution".

item.description: This is a ChatGPT-written description of our first D&D session in the 5e starter adventure, The Lost Mine of Phandelver.

item.description: Square Enix is coming out with a sourcebook and game master's guide for a table top version of Final Fantasy XIV in May. I wonder what playing that would be like...

item.description: Baldur's Gate 3 didn't really grab me until the evening I woke up to find Astarion's teeth inches from my neck...

item.description: We brought four new characters to the table for Game Night. Three of them were a hit. One... didn't get any love.

item.description: Racing a band of mercenaries and an exploding volcano in a desperate bid to save the dinos of Isla Nublar.

item.description: It's not really hard to defeat Mewtwo if everyone is on the same page. I did it both times with Mew; once built for support, once built for damage.

item.description: This weekend in glorious Hartford, Connecticut. Could I be more motivated?

item.description: Our crafty band of l'Cie defeat the big bad for the second time and head back to Cocoon to either save or destroy the floating world.

item.title: Blue Beetle
item.description: It's a superhero movie that says "Family!" more times than Dom Toretto on a bender. Worth your time?

item.title: World Yo-Yo Contest 2023: Life is the Medium, Content is the Point
item.description: YouTube decided I needed to learn about the World Yo-Yo Contest today. Okay.

item.title: The Games that Defined Me, Part 2 -- Arcade
item.description: In the 80s, I went to the arcade two or three times a week. I took my love of arcade games to home consoles.

item.title: From Hydaelyn to Veeshan: A Tribute to Creators
item.description: It's creator appreciation week, and I'm appreciating the creation myths of the most popular MMOs in the world!

item.title: Jurassic World: The Legacy of Isla Nublar
item.description: Today is the day we evacuated Isla Nublar. But, the adventure isn't yet over.

item.title: My Non-Controversial Take on the AI Revolution
item.description: At least, I haven't heard anyone contravene my take, yet.

item.title: FFXIII: Welcome to Gran Pulse
item.description: After fifteen years or so, I finally beat Berthandelus in Final Fantasy XIII, and can finally enter the second half of the game.

item.title: A Cheater's Guide to Wordle, Waffle and Squaredle
item.description: There's a really good reason why I solve these daily puzzles in full view of my family, at the kitchen table, now. It's to stop me from cheating.

item.title: Only Mew can stop Mewtwo now...
item.description: Mewtwo is coming to Pokemon Scarlet and Violet in a 7 star Tera raid. I've had exactly zero luck winning any 7 star raids so far. This time, I plan to be prepared.

item.title: Games That Defined Me (Part 1)
item.description: It's an interesting question, stated a few different ways -- 10 games to know me, or games that defined me. The question has been bouncing around Mastodon for a few days. Here's mine.

item.title: Sam Portsmouth Bridges
item.description: I really don't know what else you would call a blog post on a gaming blog about bridges in Portsmouth, NH. It's introduction week, so get ready.

item.title: The Legend of Windfyre
item.description: I'm off to New Hampshire for the day, so here's a story about Windfyre, the dragon that lives in New Hampshire's White Mountains.

item.title: I killed a wolf and all I got was this stupid broken claw
item.description: Why do you have to kill a hundred deer to get one antler? There's got to be a better way.

item.title: Adventures in Bad Game Development
item.description: I've never been able to make a good game, but I think I could make some bad ones if I just put a little effort into them.

item.title: Palia First Impressions: A Promising Start to a Social MMO Adventure
item.description: If you were waiting for the second coming of Free Realms, Palia ain't it. But, if you hate adventuring and love gathering and crafting in your MMO of choice, you might just find a new home in Palia, the new social MMO from Singularity 6.

item.title: (Not) Taming the Backlog
item.description: If I die, let it be beneath a mound of unplayed video games. Might as well accept the inevitable, right?

item.title: Speedrunning the Apocalypse
item.description: Remember way back when when social media was a way to bring together and the internet was fun and exciting? Yeah. Me, too. Let's find a way to get back there.

item.title: This Day In Blogging: August 1, 2006
item.description: I've been blogging for 25 years. Here's the things I was blogging about on the first day of August in 1998-ish, 2006, 2008, 2012, and 2020. Enjoy!

item.title: OMG it's that time again already!
Pokémon Legends: Arceus
item.description: Step into an ancient and treacherous world where Pokémon reign as wild and untamed forces, embark on a perilous quest, and discover why Pokémon Legends: Arceus delivers the most daring and immersive Pokémon experience yet!

item.title: White Knight Chronicles -- best RPG I never heard of?
item.description: White Knight Chronicles, a 2009 PS3 RPG exclusive, shut down its servers a decade ago, leaving it with only half a game. But that half a game is deeply customizable and surprisingly fun.

item.title: Is Final Fantasy XVI worth playing?
item.description: A gripping story, a deeply customizable combat system and a beautiful world being killed by its crystals. It's Final Fantasy. But is it for you?

item.title: The Official, Scientific, 2023 List of Popular MMORPGs
item.description: Here we go again. The top 38 MMOs in the last 30 days in the USA, as reported by Google Trends.

item.title: To G'raha, whom I shall never, ever, see again
item.description: My heart aches for the loss of G'raha Tia, as I wonder what could have been between us.

item.title: Slamming the Diablo.
item.description: The Diablo series evolved from dungeon crawler to dungeon brawler. Diablo IV brings it one step further...

item.title: FFXIV: How I Ruined a Realm Reborn...
item.description: I really wanted to title this post, "FFXIV: Who Hurt You To Make You Do This?". I didn't, because I knew the answer. It was me. I'm to blame.

item.title: Contact is a bad movie. And I am angry.
Horizon Forbidden West: Aloy Finds Love
PokeReport: Giovanni defeated, and some raids...
item.description: Okay, what is actually UP with that Latias raid?

item.title: Pokémon: The New(ish) Addiction
item.description: With both Pokémon Go and Pokémon Sword & Shield going on, it's pretty much peak Pokémon at the moment.

item.title: Cyberpunk 2077 vs Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet
item.description: When anyone thinks of gun-centric shooter RPGs, they naturally think first of Cyberpunk 2077 and Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet. Which is the best bang for your bullet?

item.title: If I buy an NFT, that art is mine now. Right?
item.description: Of course not. Why do people even think that?

item.title: I Was Trapped in a Closet and Forced to Play Skyrim
item.description: I mean, there must be some reason why I was unable to play any other game until I played through Skyrim and all three of its expansions...

item.title: No kidding: I bought (and sold) an NFT.
item.description: Turns out there might be at least one use case for the things.

item.title: The Order of the Stick: The Dungeon of Dorukan
item.description: Ever like a web comic that satirizes Dungeons and Dragons so much that you wanted to play as those characters in a board game? You're in luck -- "The Order of the Stick: The Dungeon of Dorukan" is here.

item.title: Skyrim, except it's a board game
item.description: We've been really enjoying this board game version of Skyrim the last couple of family game nights... but maybe it is a little TOO faithful to the videogame?

item.title: Advent of Code Day 25 -- Full of Hot Air
item.description: That's a wrap -- a Christmas wrap! -- on Advent of Code 2022!

item.title: Advent of Code Day 24 -- Blizzard Basin
item.description: The Christmas elves don't like the snow for some reason? They have the wrong boss, I think. Well, we better help them get through those couple hundred blizzards.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 23 -- Unstable Diffusion
item.description: ...because Stable Diffusion was already used, I guess. This is the annual cellular automata puzzle. Plus, why no puzzle 22?

item.title: Advent of Code Day 21 -- Monkey Math
item.description: Hey, monkeys again! Shouting! Luckily, we have an elephant to interpret. I feel... morally uneasy about today's solution.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 20 -- Grove Positioning System
item.description: As someone remarked today on Reddit, the entire plan hinged upon you remembering a ten digit prime number you randomly overheard twenty days ago.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 19 -- Not Enough Minerals
item.description: Okay, this one took a long time. I knew what to do, but what to do was super slow.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 18 -- Boiling Boulders
item.description: We're about to start on Christmas Week, that week when Advent of Code's most iconic puzzles are released. But today, we're dealing with the aftermath of an exploding volcano.

item.title: Woody, the Tetris-playing Woodpecker
item.description: True story of how a Tetris-playing bird made humanity examine its role in the universe. Plus: an exclusive interview with Woody about his marriage to a human woman!

item.title: Advent of Code Day 17 -- Pyroclastic Flow
item.description: They say "Pyroclastic Flow", but it's really Tetris. Rock Tetris. With 100,000,000 pieces. Massive.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 16 -- Proboscidea Volcanium
item.description: Trying to save elephants trapped in an erupting volcano? Sure. My solution worked for my input but not that of other people's, so let's try something a little different.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 12 -- Hill Climbing Algorithm
item.description: The title says it all. It's a "shortest path" puzzle, you're meant to use Dijkstra's algorithm, and the puzzle has no curve balls to toss at you.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 15 -- Beacon Exclusion Zone
item.description: This is the kind of puzzle I hate. Puzzles where even the best approach seems to take forever and it's hard to wrap my head around the solution.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 14 -- Regolith Reservoir
item.description: I thought, for my vacation, I'd have time to really dive deep into these puzzles. Instead, I've been buried deep in sand -- much like the hapless victim in today's puzzle.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 11 -- Monkey in the Middle
item.description: I thought this was just a puzzle to see if I knew about modulo arithmetic... but then it turned into a puzzle about VERY LARGE NUMBERS! Also, why do I bother with Java?

item.title: Advent of Code Day 10 -- Cathode-Ray Tube
item.description: Turns out the elves just really loved the Atari 2600 so much, they built their little handheld computers around them. And now it's up to us to fix one.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 9 -- Rope Bridge
item.description: The elves are crossing a bridge, but it is a really short bridge -- in fact it is the shortest bridge possible. So what did the protagonist fall into when it snapped?

item.title: Advent of Code Day 8 -- Tree Top Treehouse
item.description: It's all about taking a break from those busy reindeer games to build a treehouse with a really stellar view. Also, what is code golf?

item.title: Advent of Code Day 7 -- No Space Left On Device
item.description: Today's coding challenge was, "Make a file system without making a file system!" I failed that task.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 6 -- Tuning Trouble
item.description: What is probably the first puzzle in the theme for the rest of Advent of Code started today... with the easiest puzzle so far.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 5 -- Supply Stacks
item.description: The promised difficulty boost for Day 5 failed to materialize, instead being largely a puzzle about reading formatted input.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 4 -- Camp Cleanup
item.description: As well as my Day 4 solutions in Python and Java, I talk about the AI solving controversy in Advent of Code, and teach the chat bot to speak a language I just now invented.

item.title: Advent of Code Day 3 -- Rucksack Reorganization
Advent of Code 2022 Day 2 -- Rock Paper Scissors
Advent of Code 2022: Day 1 -- Calorie Counting
Crypto, Crypto, what will you do?
item.description: This song tells the story of a pup named Crypto who dreams of becoming wealthy through trading on various cryptocurrency markets.

item.title: A Country Song about Today's News -- November 28, 2022
item.description: It's a country song, about the news of today, written by AI!

item.title: Advent of Code 2022 and other exciting news you should totally read!
item.description: Advent of Code 2022 is just around the corner, and with it comes new challenges, new technologies, and new ways to have fun programming. Plus, Tactics Ogre: Reborn is almost finished, and Cyberpunk 2077 is waiting for me to dive in. And don't forget about the Gotham Knight Knews update, featuring Starro for four-player coop, and the new GPT-3 language model. And exclusive Guardians of the Galaxy Christmas Special spoilers!

item.title: Dive into the world of Tactics Ogre with this remake of a classic
item.description: The original Tactics Ogre returns, updated and improved for a new generation. Any fan of tactical strategy games like Fire Emblem or Final Fantasy Tactics should really take a look at the game that inspired them all.

item.title: The Best Object Removal Apps for Perfect Screenshots
item.description: If you've ever taken a screenshot only to find that it's ruined by UI elements or other elements, you know how frustrating it can be. But there are several ways to remove those elements and create the perfect image. Read on for free apps that will get the results you want with none of the stuff you don't.

item.title: Gotham Knights - The Best Superhero Game You're Not Playing
item.description: Gotham Knights is the perfect game for Batman fans who want an open world game with a good story.

item.title: Coloring between the lines... with AI
Ways that the discovery of human telepathy is changing how game shows work
item.description: I just thought this was funny. I asked GPT-3 for some help :-)

item.title: Steelrising: A Souls-Like for Revolutionary Spirits
item.description: Aegis, a living automaton, is on a quest to free France from Mad King Louis XVI's tyranny in this dark souls-like game set during the French Revolution.

item.title: Dissidia 012 Final Fantasy comes highly recommended
My One-Sided Argument with Raid: Shadow Legend's Marketing Department
Star Ocean: The Divine Force: The Demo: The Review, but what if it was written like Dear Abby?
Star Ocean: The Divine Force: The Demo
item.description: Star Ocean: The Divine Force is an upcoming action RPG with a flying combat drone and a focus on disabling enemies through battle effects. The demo offers a taste of the game's fast-paced combat and linear storytelling.

item.title: Review: Braking Day by Adam Oyebanji
item.description: Oyebanji's Braking Day is a refreshing take on the generation starship story that avoids many of the tropes of the genre. The novel follows trainee engineer Ravi MacLeod as he discovers that the truth about the 132 year journey to Tau Ceti is not what he has been told. Although the book has a few flaws, Oyebanji's writing is enjoyable and makes for a quick read.

item.title: Drakkhen for the Super Nintendo: An Unexpected Gem
Retro World Expo 2022 -- Hartford, CT
item.description: Retro World Expo in Hartford came up light on Sega Saturn games, but I got a couple new games to try. Terra isn't a fan and is warning me away from Drakkhen for the SNES.

item.title: Final Fantasy VI Pixel Remaster: Completed!
Game Night: Flamecraft
item.description: Flamecraft is a light-hearted game in which players take on the role of Flamekeepers, tasked with helping Artisan dragons create a variety of goods using their specialized flames. The game is easy to pick up and play, with players taking turns visiting shops, placing dragons, and casting enchantments to earn rewards..

item.title: The Life of a Medical Student Who Sold Himself as an NFT to Pay for School
item.description: An AI-written article about someone who sold himself as an NFT to pay for medical school, but ended up trapped.

item.title: Cid's Legacy: A Story of the World of Ruin
item.description: A year after Kefka woke the Warring Triad and destroyed the world, Celes wakes up from a coma to find that she is on an island with Cid. Cid has been sick for a long time, and Celes is determined to take care of him.

item.title: The Battle for Locke's Heart: The Remake
item.description: More Celes/Terra/Locke fanfic, I'm afraid. I gave TerraChat a lot more background on the characters and their motivations, than before.

item.title: How I used AI to write a Final Fantasy VI chatbot
item.description: GPT-3's real power comes when it can use AI to help people find what they need. In this case, information about Final Fantasy VI. Read on to find out how I used AI to help make a FF6 chatbot -- TerraChat.

item.title: The Battle for Locke's Heart: A Final Fantasy VI Fan Fic
item.description: A fan-fic written by my TerraChat FF6 bot. In this episode, Celes and Terra both love Locke, but he chooses Terra. They eventually become friends. And then it all falls to pieces.

item.title: Creating "32 Bullets" -- an AI adventure.
item.description: In "32 Bullets", you play as a professional hitman with only 32 bullets to complete 32 contracts. The catch is that you must complete them all within a certain amount of time.

item.title: The Golden Years: 30 Something
item.description: The Golden Years: 30 Something is a life simulation game that highlights the importance of balancing work, life, and everything in between. The game features multiple different endings depending on the choices you make throughout the game.

item.title: Blaugust 2022: Staying (un)Motivated
item.description: Keeping a blog alive is a lot of pressure. Wouldn't it make more sense to have a central Blaugust blog that everyone could post in, rather than having a hundred small blogs?

item.title: Leap Year: the 29th Day
item.description: After the world disappears on Leap Day, you must team up with the only other person left awake to find out what happened.

item.title: The 28th Amendment -- a game about the USA Constitution
The 27th Chamber: A Game about Time
1926: The Golden Age of Aviation
item.description: Experience the golden age of aviation in this flight simulator set in the 1920s.

item.title: Blaugust 2022: Dev Appreciation Week
item.description: We highlight some of the great turn-based RPGs that are currently in development by independent developers. These are games that are being made with passion and care, and it shows in the quality of the games. If you're a fan of turn-based RPGs, be sure to check out these games!

item.title: Blaugust 2022: Introduce Yourself week
item.description: Welcome to Blaugust week 1, where I introduce myself! Or some version of myself, anyway.

item.title: 25 Cents: The Age of the Arcade
item.description: In 25 Cents, players take on the role of arcade owners in the 1980s, running their businesses and competing against each other to attract high scores from players who drop quarters into virtual machines.

item.title: 24/7: Attention, Undead Shoppers edition.
item.description: Do you have what it takes to keep the undead under control? Find out in 24/7, a challenging and exciting first-person shooter with a horror twist. Patrol the mall at night and keep the creatures of the night in check. Use a variety of different weapons and tools to help you in your task. Can you survive until dawn?

item.title: 23 Fathoms: A Cold War Era Submarine Simulator
The 5 Best MMOs to Play in 2022
item.description: With so many MMOs on the market, it can be hard to decide which one is right for you. Here are the 5 best MMOs to play in 2022 to help you make your decision!

item.title: "The 22nd Letter": A Game of Mystery and Scrabble
item.description: The 22nd Letter is a game of mystery and Scrabble. As you play the game, you'll gradually uncover the story of "V", a man who disappeared during a Scrabble tournament long ago. Can you solve the mystery?

item.title: The Wild 20s - The City is Your Sandbox
item.description: "The Wild 20s" is an open-world sandbox game set in the Roaring Twenties. You can explore the city, find jobs, start businesses, get into trouble, or just enjoy the nightlife. It's up to you!

item.title: Can You Make It To The End Of 19th Street In This Frantic Endless Runner?
Brown Dust? Did you mean Brave Nine?
There is No Room 17 in Kaisei Academy
item.description: "There Is No Room 17 in Kaisei Academy" is a fun and mystery-filled visual novel that is perfect for fans of the genre.

item.title: The Escape of King Louis XVI
The 15th Element: A Squad-Based Shooter With Puzzle Elements
item.description: The 15th Element is a squad-based first person shooter with puzzle elements, set in an alien world. The game includes a variety of DLC options, such as a new enemy race and a survival-style mode.

item.title: The 14 Pages of Sarah Woods
item.description: The Number 14: A Touching Game with Excellent Writing and Interesting Characters

item.title: Thirteen Step Program: A Psychological Thriller
item.description: You play as a young woman who is struggling with addiction. After hitting rock bottom, you enter into recovery and start attending AA meetings. However, you quickly discover that not all is as it seems within the group…

item.title: Trial by Wombat? Not in my Mastodon!
Twelve Angry Zoos
item.description: "Twelve Angry Zoos" is a fast-paced arcade game that will test your reflexes and puzzle-solving skills. With 12 different zoos in 12 different cities, there's plenty of gameplay to keep you entertained.

item.title: 11/11/11: The Thousand Year Second
item.description: “11/11/11: The Thousand Year Second” is a turn-based strategy game about time, relationships, and choices.

item.title: 10 Million B.C.: An open-world action RPG set in the time of the dinosaurs
Game Night, by Shakespeare, Renoir and OpenAI
The Nine-Lives Chronicles
item.description: You play as a young woman who discovers she has nine lives. Along with eight other people who also have nine lives, you must use your extra lives to help others and make the world a better place.

item.title: 8 Bit Love - The Princess and the Plumber
item.description: Join Mario, Link, and Dirk in their fight to save Princess Toadstool from a life of misery with Bowser. Will they be able to get through to her? Or will she stay with the monster she loves? Find out in this heart-pounding adventure!

item.title: Fast and Furious 2099 -- an AI written final chapter to the F&F franchise.
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-3 writes the final chapter in the Fast and Furious saga -- Fast and Furious 2099.

item.title: The Amazing Adventures of 8-Ball!
item.description: "Adventure of 8-Ball" is a 2D platformer mobile game with 50 levels, attractive graphics, and catchy music. The player controls 8-ball, a little black cat, on a quest to save their owner from the evil dog catcher who has kidnapped them.

item.title: GPT-3 interviews me about Stray.
item.description: For a game where AIs rule the world, I thought it would be fun to ask an AI to interview me about my experiences playing "Stray"...

item.title: The Seven Sages: A Masterclass in Metroidvania Design
item.description: In "The Seven Sages", you play as one of the seven ancient sages of mystery, who must journey through a vast and dangerous world to gather the seven magical artifacts that will save the realm from a great evil.

item.title: The Six Cats of Madame Miracle
item.description: The Six Cats of Madame Miracle is a light-hearted and fun visual novel that is perfect for players of all ages. The puzzles are easy to learn and solve, and the game's cute art style and cheerful music make it a joy to play.
